playbooks.title: Restore/session drift triage
playbooks.summary: The restored UI is trying to hydrate against missing runtime state. Confirm whether the persisted tab/session metadata is stale before changing network or SDK code.

commands.label: Preview plugin reset plan
commands.summary: Preview the generic plugin-local reset before comparing clean and restored state behavior.

commands.label: Generate a doctor fix plan
commands.summary: Capture missing build/deploy/CLI/CDP prerequisites and write a reviewable fix script next to the doctor report.

actions: Compare persisted restore metadata with the runtime catalog the plugin is querying after reload.
Decide whether missing runtime state is expected after a cold start and should degrade gracefully.
Use reset-state or a clean vault snapshot to separate stale local state from real code regressions.

playbooks.title: Visible UI blocked by backend readiness
playbooks.summary: The plugin shell can render earlier than the backend becomes ready. Separate shell readiness from post-render enrichment and measure them independently.

commands.label: Run doctor before profiling
commands.summary: Catch missing prerequisites before trusting backend-readiness or view-open timings.

commands.label: Rebuild the HTML report
commands.summary: Produce a portable diagnosis report before comparing warm/cold timing classes.

actions: Keep visible shell state writes synchronous, but move backend snapshots and enrichments to background refresh where safe.
Record startup-complete, view-open, backend-ready, and interactive-ready milestones in the same run.
Save a warm-start baseline so cold-start backend cost does not hide frontend regressions.

playbooks.title: Artifact capture recovery
playbooks.summary: Screenshot or DOM artifacts are missing, so the run is not trustworthy enough for UI debugging yet.

commands.label: Retry CLI screenshot capture
commands.summary: Capture a fresh screenshot directly through the active Obsidian CLI command.

commands.label: Retry CLI DOM capture
commands.summary: Re-query the suspected plugin root selector before trusting downstream UI assertions.

commands.label: Capture UI through CDP
commands.summary: Fallback capture path when CLI screenshot or DOM collection still misses the active plugin leaf.

actions: Verify the selector targets the plugin pane instead of the previously active leaf.
Use a scenario or CDP post-reload eval to focus/open the plugin view before capture.
Do not trust downstream assertions until both DOM and screenshot capture succeed.
